evidence: "It's a devastating 'portrait of the artist as a young man.'"
"It's possible that Ironweed the Pulitzer Prize-winning novel was unfilmable."
"The film is comprised of focused sequences, punctuated by close-ups of each of the four women that dissolve into blood red as the 'cries and whispers' of their psyches haunt the soundtrack."
"This is an unrelentingly bleak and slow 2 1/2-hour film."
"It's a very basic video with less than ideal production values."
"I wanted to see the performances AND the artistry of cinematographer Sven Nykvist--which is ruined in this cropped version."
"All of the performances are outstanding, as is the design, cinematography and script--and it's rare these days to see such mature, nuanced and intelligent filmmaking."
"For Bergman devotees like me, any morsel tossed our way by the great master is a treat."
"Bergman is reacting to many things here--including his and his nation's own mixed feelings of neutrality and complicity during WWII."
"You leave this film feeling you've been taken somewhere you've never been before."
"It's Ullmann's performance that overcomes the contrivance."
"I was moved to tears in the epilogue when Marianne reveals that following her time with Johann and his family, she truly touched her institutionalized daughter for the first time."

summary: The user has a strong preference for complex, character-driven dramas, particularly those by Ingmar Bergman. They value high production quality and emotional depth, often seeking films that evoke strong feelings and introspection.
